summary: Structural refactor (not in-place wrap). Added Core/Application/ContinueSubtitleKind.swift (Sendable, Equatable, Codable, CaseIterable enum with 6 cases). Replaced TodayContinueSourceComposer.Step.reason: String → Step.subtitleKind: ContinueSubtitleKind. Cascaded through Candidate, TodayContinueItemAssembler, and TodayContinuationRules.ContinueItem. TodayView gained a continueSubtitleLabel(for:) helper that maps each case to String(localized:). Added 6 today.continue.subtitle.* keys × 19 locales (LLM-drafted). Updated 9 test assertions across 4 test files to assert on the semantic enum, not English strings. Boundary restored: presentation copy now lives only in Features/Today, semantic intent flows through Application/Domain.
validations_passed: xcodebuild build -quiet -project owlory_xcode/Owlory.xcodeproj -scheme Owlory -configuration Debug -destination 'generic/platform=iOS Simulator' -derivedDataPath /tmp/owlory-continue-subtitle-build CODE_SIGNING_ALLOWED=NO

contract_status_changes.contract: Today Continue row subtitle localization + boundary restoration
contract_status_changes.before: TodayContinueSourceComposer (Core/Application) owned English presentation copy ('Focus', 'Due today', 'Carried forward', 'Protocol run', 'Active', 'In progress') via Step.reason: String. ContinueItem.reason: String carried the same English to the view layer. Tests asserted on English strings. Non-English users saw English row subtitles. Architecture boundary violation: presentation copy in Application layer.
contract_status_changes.after: Core/Application emits ContinueSubtitleKind (semantic enum). ContinueItem.subtitleKind: ContinueSubtitleKind carries the semantic intent. TodayView (Features/Today) owns the localized String mapping via continueSubtitleLabel(for:). Tests assert on the semantic enum cases (.focus, .dueToday, .carriedForward, .protocolRun, .active, .inProgress). 6 new today.continue.subtitle.* keys exist across all 19 locales. Boundary restored: presentation copy lives only in Features/, semantic intent flows through Application/Domain.
proof: 4 test files updated to assert on enum cases

residual_risks: Other Core/Application or Core/Domain helpers still own English copy (FocusSuggestionRules, CalibrationRules, ReadinessRules, PatternNudgeRules). Five queued slices cover those. This slice only restored the Continue subtitle boundary.

Tests now assert on enum cases. If a developer adds a new ContinueSubtitleKind case without adding a matching today.continue.subtitle.<case> key, the helper's switch is exhaustive so the compiler flags it, but the localization lookup would fall back to the raw key string. No runtime breakage, just a missing translation.

Per-locale review return files now 21 entries stale per locale (9 anchors + 6 header greeting + 6 subtitle since 2026-05-16). Run python3 Tools/localization-return-files-refresh.py --apply to refresh.
Native review remains outstanding for every locale.
The 6 subtitle keys are LLM-drafted by claude-opus-4-7. The German 'Im Gange' (In progress) is correct but conversational; native review may pick differently.
recommended_next_reason: Next Core/Domain follow-up. Smallest payload (1 message + 4 focusBalanceTitle helper duplications). focusBalanceTitle can simply delegate to existing LifeDomain.localizedDisplayName — minimal new infrastructure needed. Good warm-up before the larger ReadinessRules/CalibrationRules/FocusSuggestionRules refactors.
